heading: Determination; data collection and dissemination
label: 1421a Determination; data collection and dissemination

children.heading: Determination for release
children.label: a Determination for release
children.text: The Secretary shall, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, the Marine Mammal Commission, and individuals with knowledge and experience in marine science, marine mammal science, marine mammal veterinary and husbandry practices, and marine conservation, including stranding network participants, develop objective criteria, after an opportunity for public review and comment, to provide guidance for determining at what point a rehabilitated marine mammal is releasable to the wild.

children.label: A
children.text: rescuing and rehabilitating stranded or entangled marine mammals, including criteria used by stranding network participants, on a species-by-species basis, for determining at what point a marine mammal undergoing rescue and rehabilitation is returnable to the wild; and

children.label: B
children.text: collecting, preserving, labeling, and transporting marine mammal tissues for physical, chemical, and biological analyses;
appropriate scientific literature on marine mammal health, disease, and rehabilitation;

strandings and entanglements, including unusual mortality events, which the Secretary shall compile and analyze, by stranding region, to monitor species, numbers, conditions, and causes of illnesses and deaths of stranded marine mammals and entangled marine mammals to allow comparison of the causes of illness and deaths in stranded marine mammals and entangled marine mammals with physical, chemical, and biological environmental parameters; and
other life history and reference level data, including marine mammal tissue analyses.

children.heading: Information required to be submitted and collected
children.label: c Information required to be submitted and collected
A
children.text: data on the stranding event, including NOAA Form 89–864 (OMB #0648–0178), NOAA Form 89–878 (OMB #0648–0178), similar successor forms, or similar information in an appropriate format required by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service for species under its management authority;

children.label: i
children.text: weather and tide conditions;

children.label: ii
children.text: offshore human, predator, or prey activity;

children.label: iii
children.text: morphometrics;

children.label: iv
children.text: behavior;

children.label: v
children.text: health assessments;

children.label: vi
children.text: life history samples; or

children.label: vii
children.text: stomach and intestinal contents; and

children.label: i
children.text: histopathology;

children.label: ii
children.text: toxicology;

children.label: iii
children.text: microbiology;

children.label: iv
children.text: virology; or

children.label: v
children.text: parasitology.

children.label: A
children.text: the data described in paragraph (1)(A) not later than 30 days after the date of a response to a stranding or entanglement event;

children.label: B
children.text: the compiled data described in paragraph (1)(B) not later than 30 days after the date on which the data is available to the stranding network participant; and

children.label: C
children.text: the compiled data described in paragraph (1)(C) not later than 30 days after the date on which the laboratory analysis has been reported to the stranding network participant.

children.heading: Online data input system
children.label: 3 Online data input system
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere, in consultation with the stranding network and the Office of Evaluation Sciences of the General Services Administration, shall establish an online system for the purposes of efficient and timely submission of data described in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: to improve real-time coordination of response to stranding and entanglement events across geographic areas and between stranding coordinators;

children.label: B
children.text: to identify and quickly disseminate information on potential public health risks;

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate integrated interdisciplinary research;

children.label: D
children.text: to facilitate peer-reviewed publications;

children.label: E
children.text: to archive regional data into 1 national database for future analyses; and

children.label: F
children.text: for education and outreach activities.

children.label: A
children.text: by staff of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration or the United States Fish and Wildlife Service that responded directly to a stranding or entanglement event is available to the public through the Health MAP and the Observation System not later than 30 days after that data or metadata is collected by, available to, or reported to the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: by a stranding network participant that responded directly to a stranding or entanglement event is made available to the public through the Health MAP and the Observation System 2 years after the date on which that data are submitted to the Secretary under subsection (c).

children.heading: Written release
children.label: A Written release
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2)(B), the Secretary may make data described in paragraph (2)(B) publicly available earlier than 2 years after the date on which that data are submitted to the Secretary under subsection (c), if the stranding network participant has completed a written release stating that such data may be made publicly available.

children.heading: Law enforcement
children.label: B Law enforcement
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the Secretary may withhold data for a longer period than the period of time described in paragraph (2) in the event of a law enforcement action or legal action that may be related to that data.

make publicly available guidance about uniform data and metadata standards to ensure that data collected in accordance with this section can be archived in a form that is readily accessible and understandable to the public through the Health MAP and the Observation System; and
periodically update such guidance.

children.heading: Management policy
children.label: f Management policy
children.text: In collaboration with the regional stranding networks, the Secretary shall develop, and periodically update, a data management and public outreach collaboration policy for stranding or entanglement events.

children.heading: Authorship agreements and acknowledgment policy
children.label: g Authorship agreements and acknowledgment policy
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere, shall include authorship agreements or other acknowledgment considerations for use of data by the public, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: h Savings clause
children.text: The Secretary shall not require submission of research data that are not described in subsection (c).
